title: Apple Succession Plan Emerges as Company Turns 50
summary:
Interviews with Apple insiders reveal a growing consensus that John Ternus is the leading contender to become the company's next CEO. Mark Gurman has more.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: New Zealand Yields Rise to Highest in a Year on Outlook Cut, Oil
summary: New Zealand bond yields rose to the highest in about a year after the country’s AA+ credit rating outlook was cut to negative by Fitch Ratings.

title: Fonterra Raises Guidance as Conflict Poses Risk to Supply Chain
summary: Fonterra Cooperative Group, the world’s biggest dairy exporter, lifted full-year guidance while warning that the Middle East conflict poised a risk to supply chains in the second half.

title: Traders Brace for Turbulent Open as War Rages On: Markets Wrap
summary: Investors are bracing for another turbulent session as the US war in Iran enters a fourth week with no signs of easing.

title: Poste Launches €10.8 Billion Offer to Delist Telecom Italia
summary: Poste Italiane SpA said it will launch a voluntary public offer valued at about €10.8 billion ($12.5 billion) for Telecom Italia SpA, seeking full control of the former phone monopoly and setting up its delisting.
